mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equity cash flows, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS declines for two consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign of the signal is negative in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening household income and demand, which should reduce near-term equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average forward return after the UNRATE threshold is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening final demand and labor income growth, which lifts revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition PAYEMS up and RSAFS up does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-improvement episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO with stable or lower CPIAUCSL implies stronger real activity without binding inflation pressure, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through higher expected earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with flat or falling CPIAUCSL do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if inflationary episodes reverse the sign of the effect.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts HOUST break upward from a weak base, the signal captures an early-cycle demand turn that leads equity prices over the next 5 trading days through improved growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST threshold events are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% compresses equity valuation multiples and raises recession risk, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 50% in tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in PAYEMS and a rising UNRATE identifies labor-market slack that reduces earnings visibility and should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal is weaker than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery, accelerating retail sales RSAFS together with rising industrial activity INDPRO indicates synchronized demand normalization, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in a clear majority of cases, or if the effect disappears outside recovery regimes.
